rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant General Cable Corporation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 20, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant General Cable Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR POPE ET AL VS. KAISER GYPSUN COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 20, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Intervenors on behalf of defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Intervenors failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Pope was exposed to asbestos-containing Rich-Tex products. Mr. Pope's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to intervenors' special interrogatories preclude intervenors from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. Intervenors also failed to submit evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The declaration of Richard Watkins is insufficient to sustain their burden because intervenors failed to demonstrate that Mr. Pope encountered Rich-Tex products only during the time when they were asbestos-free. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
